statement.p: Currently, an artist can sell 270 paintings every year at the price of {\$100.00} per painting. Each time he raises the price per painting by {\$5.00}, he sells 5 fewer paintings every year.

Assume he will raise the price per painting x times, then he will sell 270-5x paintings every year at the price of 100+5x dollars. His yearly income can be modeled by the equation: i=(100+5x)(270-5x) where i stands for his yearly income in dollars. If the artist wants to earn {\$33{,}325.00} per year from selling paintings, what new price should he set?
To earn {\$33{,}325.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is per painting, and the higher price is per painting.

solution.p: If the artist wants to make {\$33{,}325.00} per year, the equation becomes:
We will change the equation to standard form:
Next, we can solve this quadratic equation with the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=-25,b=850,c=-6325.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] x \amp = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\ x \amp = \frac{-(850) \pm \sqrt{(850)^{2}-4(-25)(-6325)}}{2(-25)} \\ x \amp = \frac{-850 \pm \sqrt{90000}}{-50} \\ x \amp = \frac{-850 \pm 300}{-50} \\ x\amp =\frac{-850 + 300}{-50} \text{ or } x=\frac{-850 - 300}{-50} \\ x\amp =11 \text{ or } x=23 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: If the artist raises the price 11 times, the new price would be 100+5\cdot11=155 dollars per painting.
If the artist raises the price 23 times, the new price would be 100+5\cdot23=215 dollars per painting.
To earn {\$33{,}325.00} per year, the artist could sell his paintings at two different prices. The lower price is {\$155.00} per painting, and the higher price is {\$215.00} dollars per painting.
